channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Love cilantro? Enough to bathe in it? Chipotle has you covered
item.description: Chipotle is ringing in the holiday season by reviving its gift set featuring fan-created items inspired by the restaurant.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, KTLA 5

item.title: Will a winter storm hit the US over Thanksgiving week? Here's what forecasts show so far
item.description: WINDSOR, Calif. (AP) — Forecasters warned over the weekend that another round of winter weather could complicate travel leading up to Thanksgiving in parts of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GODOFREDO A. VASQUEZ and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A man called 911 for help during a home invasion. Las Vegas police fatally shot him
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A Las Vegas man called for police help during a home invasion before an officer fatally shot him, according to authorities and 911 calls. Brandon Durham called 911 early Nov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Civil Air Patrol plane crash during Colorado training exercise leaves 2 dead, 1 injured
item.description: A Civil Air Patrol Cessna 182 crashed in a mountainous area of Larimer County, Colorado, on Saturday. Two people were killed and one was "severely injured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jason Kelce's wife, Kylie, announces she is pregnant with the couple's fourth child
item.description: Former Philadelphia Eagles center Jason Kelce's wife announced Friday that she's pregnant with the couple's fourth child.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about Brooke Rollins, Trump's pick for agriculture secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has selected former White House aide Brooke Rollins to lead the Department of Agriculture in his second administration. Here are some things to know about Trump’s choice and the agency that Rollins would lead if she is confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Here are the people Trump has picked for key positions so far
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is filling key posts in his second administration, and it's shaping up much differently than his first. He's prioritizing loyalists for top jobs. Trump was bruised and hampered by internal squabbles during his initial term in office.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump taps Rollins as agriculture chief, completing proposed slate of Cabinet secretaries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday that he will nominate former White House aide Brooke Rollins to be his agriculture secretary, the last of his picks to lead executive agencies and another choice from within his established circle of advisers and allies.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the most heated Thanksgiving food fights
item.description: Thanksgiving dinner may be one of the most anticipated meals of the year for many people, but it is also one of the most debated. From the correct way to prepare the turkey to the best side dishes and desserts, here are the dishes worth fighting over this Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO head and Trump meet in Florida for talks on global security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. President-elect Donald Trump and the head of NATO have met for talks on global security, the military alliance said Saturday. In a brief statement, NATO said Trump and its secretary general, Mark Rutte, met on Friday in Palm Beach, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy ‘DOGE’ confidence in Supreme Court may be tested
item.description: Musk and Ramaswamy will head a nongovernmental commission to dismantle government bureaucracy and cut costs.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Voters rejected historic election reforms across the US, despite more than $100M push
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Two weeks before Election Day, activists from across the country gathered for an online rally heralding the historic number of state ballot initiatives seeking to change the way people vote.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners who let nearly 190 bodies decay plead guilty to corpse abuse
item.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. (AP) — The owners of a Colorado funeral home who let nearly 190 bodies decay in a room-temperature building and gave grieving families fake ashes pleaded guilty on Friday to corpse abuse.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee: Hostages must be released
item.description: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee criticized the U.S. on Friday for pressuring Israel instead of holding Hamas accountable.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Texas education board approves optional Bible-infused curriculum for elementary schools
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas’ education board voted Friday to allow Bible-infused teachings in elementary schools under optional new curriculum that could test boundaries between religion and public classrooms in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Youngkin announces order to protect children from social media
item.description: Governor Glenn Youngkin issued an executive order on Thursday in the hopes of protecting children from addictive social media platforms.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, NewsNation

item.title: Teen suspect arrested after cheerleader found dead in woods
item.description: Tennessee cheerleader Savannah Copeland, 13, was allegedly stabbed to death by a 15-year-old after the pair met up around midnight Oct. 22.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officer kills pet dog mistaken for a coyote in Massachusetts town. The owner says it was unnecessary
item.description: An animal control officer shot and killed a pet dog in a Massachusetts town after mistaking it for a coyote in an incident local police are describing as a sad mix-up.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court steps into fight over FCC's $8 billion subsidies for internet and phone services
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday stepped into a major legal fight over the $8 billion a year the federal government spends to subsidize phone and internet services in schools, libraries and rural areas, in a new test of federal regulatory power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas approves optional Bible-based lessons for public schools
item.description: Texas gave final approval Friday to optional biblical lesson plans for kindergarten through 5th grade classes in state public schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman in coma after trying to save pro golfer's 6-year-old son from drowning
item.description: Professional golfer Brian Harman revealed that a close family friend has been in a coma for weeks after trying to save his 6-year-old son from a near-drowning.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WGN 9

item.title: How much is too much credit card debt? What experts say
item.description: The average person owns nearly four credit cards, according to Experian, one of the three major credit reporting bureaus. And the average debt one person owes equals more than half of the median American household income. Some may wonder if they have "too much" debt on their cards.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KTLA 5

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026 after Milton; new ballpark deal dies: Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Daniel Penny defense rests as final witness reveals Jordan Neely had open warrant, defendant doesn't testify
item.description: Expert witness for the defense, Dr. Satish Chundru, returns to the stand, and Daniel Penny could also testify at his New York City subway chokehold trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family of woman who went missing on cross-country vacation says cryptic texts sent from phone unlike her
item.description: Hannah Kobayashi never got on her flight from LA to NYC on Nov. 8 and disappeared in Los Angeles; new surveillance footage shows her with a mysterious man, her family says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Man convicted of killing Laken Riley sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — The Venezuelan man convicted of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole in a case that became a flashpoint in the national debate over immigration.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fugitive arrested after Chicago standoff blocks away from DNC is being returned to Mississippi
item.description: At the time of the escape, the fugitive was set to make a court appearance on Mississippi charges of attempted murder and armed robbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Climate change goosed hurricane wind strength by 18 mph since 2019, study says
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — Human-caused climate change made Atlantic hurricanes about 18 miles per hour (29 kilometers per hour) stronger in the last six years, a new scientific study found Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC
